title: erophila verna subsp. krockeri
intro: Erophila verna subsp. krockeri, also known as the early winter-cress or common winter-cress, is a plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Erophila verna subsp. krockeri are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
